content_body:
Nestled in the heart of the North Georgia Mountains, Blue Ridge is a quaint town that offers some of the best fly fishing in the Southeast. With its pristine rivers and streams, Blue Ridge is a must-visit destination for any fly fishing enthusiast. The region's cool mountain streams are perfect for fly fishing and provide a unique experience for anglers of all skill levels.

Fishing the Toccoa River

One of the most popular fly fishing locations in Blue Ridge is the Toccoa River. The river is home to a mix of wild and stocked trout, making it a great destination for anglers of all skill levels. The Toccoa River is known for its beautiful scenery, and fishing here provides a unique opportunity to connect with nature.

Fishing Rock Creek

Another great location for fly fishing in Blue Ridge is Rock Creek. This small stream is a tributary of the Toccoa River and is home to a thriving population of wild trout. The creek is perfect for wading and provides anglers with a great opportunity to catch some beautiful fish.

Fishing Noontootla Creek

The nearby Noontootla Creek is also a great location for fly fishing in Blue Ridge, however it is guided fishing only. This creek is known for its large population of rainbow and brown trout, and anglers here have the opportunity to catch some trophy-sized fish. The creek is also known for its beautiful scenery and is a great location for a day trip with a guide.

If you're planning a fly fishing trip to Blue Ridge, Georgia, you may want to consider hiring a local guide. A professional guide can help you navigate the local waters, improve your technique, and increase your chances of catching some beautiful fish.

Best Fly Fishing Guides in North GA

NORTH GEORGIA TROUT FISHING

Joe DiPietro is a highly experienced fly fishing guide based in Blue Ridge, Georgia. He has been guiding anglers for over 30 years and has a deep knowledge of the local waters and fish species.

Joe's focus is on providing his clients with a memorable and enjoyable fly fishing experience, while also promoting the conservation and preservation of the natural resources in the area. He is committed to practicing catch-and-release fishing and follows strict ethical guidelines to ensure the sustainability of the local fish populations.

Joe offers guided fly fishing trips on a variety of local waters, including the Toccoa River, Noontootla Creek, and Rock Creek. He can accommodate anglers of all skill levels, from beginners to advanced fly fishermen, and provides all necessary equipment. His trips are personalized to each client's needs and interests, with a focus on teaching proper techniques and providing valuable instruction to help anglers improve their skills.

Joe is highly regarded by his clients and has received many positive reviews for his expertise, professionalism, and friendly demeanor. He is passionate about fly fishing and sharing his knowledge with others, and his dedication to providing a great experience for his clients is evident in the glowing testimonials he receives.

Joe is experienced, knowledgeable, and passionate about fly fishing. If you're looking for a guided fly fishing trip in Blue Ridge, Georgia, consider booking with North Georgia Fly Fishing for a memorable experience.

GEORGIA WILD TROUT

Georgia Wild Trout is a guide service located in Blue Ridge, Georgia, that specializes in fly fishing for wild trout in the local streams and rivers. Their guides are experienced anglers who are passionate about the sport of fly fishing and preserving the natural resources in the area.

The guides at Georgia Wild Trout are knowledgeable about the local waters and the fish species that inhabit them. They have a deep understanding of the behavior and feeding patterns of wild trout, which allows them to provide valuable insight and instruction to their clients. They can also help anglers identify the best fly patterns and techniques to use in different fishing situations.

Georgia Wild Trout offers guided fishing trips on a variety of local waters, including the Toccoa River, Noontootla Creek, and Rock Creek. They offer half-day and full-day trips, and all equipment is provided. The guides can accommodate anglers of all skill levels, from beginners to advanced fly fishermen. 

In addition to providing a great fishing experience, the guides at Georgia Wild Trout are committed to preserving the natural beauty and resources of the area. They practice catch-and-release fishing and follow strict ethical guidelines to ensure the sustainability of the local fish populations.

The guides at Georgia Wild Trout are experienced, knowledgeable, and passionate about fly fishing. If you're looking for a highly experienced and knowledgeable fly fishing guide in Blue Ridge, Georgia, Tad and his crew are an excellent choice. Their commitment to conservation, personalized approach, and passion for the sport make Georgia Wild Trout a standout fly-fishing guide in the North Georgia area.

Luxury Cabin Rentals on the Water

Browse our luxury Blue Ridge cabin rentals that are along the Toccoa River. In general, if you try to fish the Toccoa River you only have a couple of access locations that the general public uses. But if you stay in one our Toccoa River cabin rentals, you will have easy private access to the river. Check out The River's Edge, A Rolling River Cabin, Fallen Timber Lodge, Riverview Lodge, and Outlaw Ridge for private Toccoa River access.

We have two cabins along Noontootla Creek ~ Serendipity on Noontootla Creek and Cherokee Sunrise. Serendipity offers private fly fishing, catch-and-release in front of the cabin's property only. Cherokee Sunrise offers guided fly-fishing only, so hooking up with one of these outstanding North GA fly fishing guides would be an exhilarating experience.
title: Fly Fishing Guides in North GA
